passages.text: The industrial revolution was not just a period of technological innovation; it also (16) --- profound changes in the social structure. People migrated from rural areas to urban centers in search of work. However, the living conditions in these cities were (17) --- poor. (18) --- the challenges, this era laid the foundation for modern economic growth. Many historians argue that (19) --- the steam engine had not been invented, the pace of development (20) --- much slower.

The concept of 'Deep Time' was first introduced by James Hutton in the 18th century, challenging the prevailing belief that the Earth was only a few thousand years old. By observing geological formations, Hutton realized that the processes of erosion and sedimentation occurred so slowly that the Earth must be millions, if not billions, of years old. This shift in perspective was crucial for the development of evolutionary biology, as it provided the vast timescales necessary for natural selection to occur. Today, we understand Deep Time through radiometric dating, which allows scientists to pinpoint the age of rocks with remarkable precision.

The Anthropocene is a proposed geological epoch dating from the commencement of significant human impact on Earth's geology and ecosystems. While the exact starting date is still under debate, many scientists suggest the mid-20th century, coinciding with the 'Great Acceleration' of population growth and industrialization. This era is characterized by human-induced changes such as global warming, ocean acidification, and mass extinction. Critics of the term argue that the impact is not uniform across humanity and that naming the entire species as the culprit ignores the disproportionate role of industrialized nations. Despite these debates, the concept has gained traction in both scientific and humanities circles as a way to understand our current environmental crisis.

The advent of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the creative arts has sparked a heated debate regarding the nature of authorship and creativity. AI models, trained on vast datasets of human-made art, can now generate poetry, paintings, and music that are indistinguishable from human works. Proponents argue that AI is merely a tool, much like the camera was for photographers, extending human capability rather than replacing it. Conversely, skeptics fear that the automation of creativity will lead to the devaluation of human labor and the homogenization of culture. Furthermore, legal systems are struggling to keep pace, as current copyright laws are generally built around the premise of a human creator.

Neuroplasticity, the brain's ability to reorganize itself by forming new neural connections throughout life, has revolutionized our understanding of the human mind. Previously, it was believed that the brain's structure was relatively fixed after a certain age in childhood. However, research now shows that the brain continues to change in response to learning, experience, and even physical injury. This discovery has profound implications for the treatment of strokes and traumatic brain injuries, as well as for educational strategies. By engaging in cognitively demanding activities, individuals can essentially 'rewire' their brains to improve memory and cognitive function well into old age.

options.E: Significant intervention is the only way the government can cause an economic crisis.

questions.question: Few students managed to pass the exam, as the questions were far more difficult than expected.

options.A: The exam was easy, so almost every student passed it with high marks.
options.B: Because the exam was surprisingly tough, only a small number of students succeeded.
options.C: The students expected the exam to be difficult, but it was actually quite simple.
options.D: Many students passed the exam despite the fact that the questions were very hard.
options.E: The questions were so difficult that no one was able to pass the exam at all.

questions.question: The manager suggested that the employees take a short break to improve their productivity.

options.A: The manager told the employees they were not being productive enough.
options.B: The employees asked the manager for a break to help them work better.

options.D: Taking breaks is the only way employees can remain productive at work.

questions.question: Renewable energy sources such as wind and solar power have become increasingly popular over the last decade. They offer a sustainable alternative to fossil fuels, which are finite and harmful to the environment. ---. However, the transition to 100% renewable energy still faces challenges like storage and grid stability.

options.A: Coal and oil are still the most widely used energy sources globally.
options.B: Many countries are now investing heavily in these green technologies to reduce emissions.
options.C: Solar panels are becoming more expensive due to the lack of raw materials.
options.D: Wind turbines can sometimes be a threat to local bird populations.
options.E: Nuclear energy is often considered a cleaner alternative than coal.

questions.question: In the world of literature, 'magical realism' is a genre where fantastic elements are set in a realistic environment. Authors like Gabriel García Márquez used this style to explore complex political and social themes. ---. This allows readers to experience the extraordinary as if it were an everyday occurrence.

options.A: The genre first became popular in Latin America during the 20th century.
options.B: Many critics believe that magical realism is too difficult for the average reader.
options.C: In these stories, supernatural events are treated with the same matter-of-factness as daily life.
options.D: Science fiction, on the other hand, usually takes place in the distant future.
options.E: The use of metaphors is essential for any successful piece of fiction.

questions.question: The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system, stretching over 2,300 kilometers. It is home to thousands of species of marine life, making it a vital part of the ocean's ecosystem. ---. Rising sea temperatures caused by climate change lead to coral bleaching, which can be fatal for the reef.

options.A: Tourism provides a significant source of income for the local economy.
options.B: Scientists are working on ways to transplant heat-resistant coral species.
options.C: However, this delicate environment is under constant threat from global warming.
options.D: Divers from all over the world travel to Australia to see the vibrant colors.
options.E: The reef is so large that it can even be seen from outer space.

questions.question: Psychologists have long studied the effects of social media on mental health. While it can help people stay connected, excessive use has been linked to anxiety and depression. ---. This constant comparison can lead to feelings of inadequacy and low self-esteem.

options.A: Many platforms now include features that track how much time you spend online.
options.B: Users often post only the most positive aspects of their lives, creating an unrealistic standard.
options.C: Cyberbullying is another serious issue that needs to be addressed by authorities.
options.D: Digital literacy should be taught in schools to protect young children.
options.E: The benefits of social media often outweigh the negative consequences for most users.

questions.question: Urbanization is a global trend, with more than half of the world's population now living in cities. People move to urban areas in search of better jobs, education, and healthcare. ---. Cities often struggle with overcrowding, pollution, and a lack of affordable housing.

options.B: Rural areas are becoming increasingly abandoned as younger generations move away.
options.C: Yet, this rapid growth also brings about significant infrastructure challenges.
options.D: Modern architecture has transformed the skylines of many major capitals.
